title: Knotted long stitch
materials: text paper 90 gsm folded signatures (4 or 5 sheets each)
decorative thread in a contrasting colour for the knot (linen or hemp)
bookbinding-board-2mm (optional — knotted long stitch can be limp-cover too)
sources: Keith Smith, Non-Adhesive Binding (1990) — chain-link and knotted variation structures. Long-stitch binding tradition pre-dates printed books; examples survive in Ethiopian and Coptic binding tradition, described in Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901).
notes: A long-stitch binding variation where each spine stitch is finished with a decorative half-hitch knot on the outside of the spine, creating a bead-like row of knots running head to tail. Technically distinct from the plain long-stitch (which has smooth spine stitches) and the knotted-spine stitch used in some Japanese traditions. Covers: sewing station spacing for the knot beat, executing the knot (half-hitch on exit, pull snug), keeping consistent tension so knots sit evenly, the overall sewing sequence across multiple signatures.
